screenshot_notes: Both desktop (1280x800) and mobile (375x812) captured. Night owl vibe active at 20:45 UTC — 'Burning the midnight oil'. Desktop shows speech bubble 'Can we circle back on that PR?' above Bloberto's desk. Carlos visible at bottom with 1h 41m timer. Mobile shows 'Per my last commit...' bubble floating above the office floor container — overlapping the tab row area as documented. Nothing fixed since review #50.
new_observations: Zero fixes landed since review #50. Every carry-over is intact. The codebase was touched (App.jsx and Office.jsx both show Mar 9 18:53 mtime) but all flagged issues remain. Either the changes were cosmetic or they introduced something not yet visible.

The window.matchMedia issue in Office.jsx (event handler path) appears correct — line 665 is now inside handleClick, not render. That specific instance is no longer problematic. The App.jsx line 1153 instance in JSX render remains unfixed.
plantStageNames has now been declared but unused for 9 consecutive review cycles. At this point it deserves its own tombstone.
The accessibility score drops from 3.5 to 3.0. Three a11y carry-overs have been open for 3+ cycles each. That is no longer 'low priority' by any reasonable measure.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #49->#50->#51] onResume is undefined in App header — paused pill onClick is a silent no-op with two independent failures
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1151: the polling-paused-pill renders onClick={onResume}, but onResume is never defined as a variable in App component scope — it is only a prop name inside StatsBar. The actual resume handler is an anonymous function passed as StatsBar's onResume prop (line 1216) with no binding to a local variable. onClick calls undefined — React silently ignores it. ADDITIONALLY, App.css line 1020 still has pointer-events:none on .polling-paused-pill, making pointer interaction physically impossible regardless. Two independent bugs. Fix: (1) extract named callback: const handleResume = useCallback(() => { setPollingPaused(false); lastActivity.current = Date.now(); syncFromGitHub(); }, [syncFromGitHub]). (2) pass handleResume to header pill onClick and to StatsBar onResume prop. (3) remove pointer-events:none from .polling-paused-pill. (4) add role=button, tabIndex=0, cursor:pointer. Third cycle. This is the most user-visible broken interaction on the page.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #47->#48->#49->#50->#51] Mobile speech bubble covers Office tab button area at 375px
suggestions.description: Confirmed again in review #51 mobile screenshot. Bloberto's speech bubble (position:absolute, bottom:calc(100%+8px) relative to character) floats above the office floor container and overlaps the tab-toggle row on 375px viewports. The transition quote is partially occluded by the Office/Dashboard tab bar. No @media (max-width:480px) override for .speech-bubble was added. Fix: add @media (max-width:480px) rule: .speech-bubble { max-width: calc(100vw - 80px); right: -10px; left: auto; transform: none; } to keep the bubble within the floor boundary. Five cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #46->#47->#48->#49->#50->#51] WindowElement sunrise (hours 5–8) has gradient but zero decorative content
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WindowElement: isMidnight (0-4) renders stars, isDaytime (9-16) renders clouds, isGoldenHour (17-19) renders amber cloud — but sunrise (5-8) has no corresponding constant and no rendered content. The sky is a bare orange-pink gradient for 3 hours daily. Fix: add const isSunrise = hour >= 5 && hour <= 8 and render a small yellow semicircle anchored at the bottom-center of the sky div. One focused code path. Six cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #45->...#51] Activity filter logic duplicated across two JSX render sites
suggestions.description: App.jsx: the activityLog filter chain runs in TWO separate places each render — once as an IIFE for the count badge (lines 1288-1297), and once as the entries prop to <ActivityLog> (lines 1343-1356). The logic is identical. Extract to: const filteredActivity = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...type...).filter(...worker...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]) and use in both places. Seven cycles of duplicate logic.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #45->...#51] IIFE inside JSX for solo spotlight message
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1244: the solo spotlight message uses an IIFE directly in JSX — (() => { const soloKey = ...; return ... })(). Creates a new closure every render, untestable, confusing to read. Extract to const soloSpotlightMsg = SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.[teamVibe.key] ?? ... above the return statement. Seven cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] PING_REACTIONS mixed narrative voice — third-person descriptions instead of first-person replies
suggestions.description: Office.jsx PING_REACTIONS: luna='Creative review in progress...' (status report), dave='He hears you through the headphones.' (third person narrator), carlos='Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.' (imperative command), maya='Check the PRs — purple and correct as always.' (borderline), sofia='Found 3 more bugs already.' (ambiguous). The ping metaphor is a reply — should be first-person. Suggested fixes: luna: 'In review. Send snacks.', dave: 'Heard you. These headphones are not noise-cancelling.', carlos: 'It is in the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs.', maya: 'PRs are purple and correct. As always.', sofia: 'Found 3 more already. You are welcome.' Ten cycles. The character voice is otherwise excellent — this is the one rough edge.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #48->#49->#50->#51] WallClock SVG has both aria-label and title child — duplicate accessible name
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WallClock line 1027: SVG has aria-label='Wall clock showing H:M' AND a <title> child with time + vibe comment. Screen readers may announce both, or aria-label shadows the richer title. Per SVG a11y spec, <title> provides the accessible name when role=img is present — aria-label overrides it unnecessarily. Fix: remove aria-label, keep <title> with role=img. The vibe clock comments are charming — do not delete them. Four cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #49->#50->#51] Empty workers array shows after-hours office during loading at any time of day
suggestions.description: During initial load, activeWorkers=[] is passed to Office.jsx. getTeamVibeKey([]) returns 'after-hours'. Office renders with locked door, purple glow, and sleep doodle before data arrives. At 2 PM on a weekday this is visually wrong. The isLoading flag exists in App but is NOT passed as a prop to Office (line 1212: <Office workers={activeWorkers} roster={roster} isSyncing={isSyncing} ...> — no isLoading). Fix: pass isLoading prop to Office and use last-known vibe from localStorage (key: 'bloberto-vibe-key') as fallback, or initialize vibe state from localStorage before first fetch completes. Third cycle.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #50->#51] office-whiteboard--done and --urgent use hardcoded light colors in dark theme
suggestions.description: Office.css: .office-whiteboard--done sets background:#f0ffe8 and .office-whiteboard--urgent sets background:#fff0f0. Both are hardcoded light colors. In dark mode they create jarring bright rectangles on the left wall. Replace with CSS custom properties: --wb-done-bg (default: #f0ffe8, dark override: rgba(34,197,94,0.12)) and --wb-urgent-bg (default: #fff0f0, dark override: rgba(239,68,68,0.1)). Second cycle.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #49->#50->#51] vibe-weather div has aria-label but no ARIA role — accessible name is orphaned
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: div.vibe-weather has aria-label={VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS[vibe]} but no role attribute. An aria-label on a plain div is ignored by most screen readers. Fix: add role='img' to the div. This has been confirmed again in review #51 source — still no role attribute. Third cycle.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #49->#50->#51] lone-survivor-label missing aria-hidden — screen reader announces decorative 0.45rem text
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1472: the lone-survivor-label div (fontSize 0.45rem, opacity 0.6, entirely inline styles) has no aria-hidden=true. Screen readers announce it redundantly alongside the worker's accessible name. Also: this div is fully inline-styled — it should be a CSS class. Add aria-hidden='true' and move styles to .lone-survivor-label. Third cycle.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #49->#50->#51] window.matchMedia called inline in JSX render — not reactive, unsafe outside browser
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1153: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches is evaluated directly in JSX inside the polling-paused-pill render path. Not reactive — if the user connects a mouse or a touch-enabled device the label never updates. Throws in SSR/test environments. Note: the previous Office.jsx instance (line 665) was correctly moved inside an event handler and is no longer an issue. Only App.jsx line 1153 remains. Fix: const isTouch = useMemo(() => window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches, []) or use navigator.maxTouchPoints > 0. Third cycle.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #47->#48->#49->#50->#51] Footer tagline counter wraps without visual indication
suggestions.description: App.jsx footer counter shows 1/9 through 9/9 then silently wraps to 1/9 with no feedback that the user has cycled through all quotes. Fix: show a loop icon (↺) or change label to '1/9 ↺' to signal wrap-around. Five cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #48->#49->#50->#51] handleTabKeyDown not memoized — new function reference on every render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 742: handleTabKeyDown is declared as a plain arrow function, not wrapped in useCallback. It is passed to two tab button onKeyDown props. Wrap in useCallback with deps [tab, syncFromGitHub]. Four cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #48->#49->#50->#51] Ghost OOO bubble reimplements getRelativeTime inline
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 702-711: ghost character OOO bubble contains a full inline IIFE reimplementing getRelativeTime (which also lives in App.jsx line 55). This is the third utility function duplicated across files. Extract to src/utils/time.js and import in both files. Four cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #44->...#51] Coffee corner chat label truncates on 320px viewports
suggestions.description: Office.css: .coffee-corner__chat-label and .coffee-corner__solo-label have no overflow protection. On 320px viewports the text ('☕ Name1 & Name2') overflows the corner box. Add overflow:hidden; text-overflow:ellipsis; white-space:nowrap; max-width:100% to both label classes and reduce font-size at max-width:380px. Eight cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #46->...#51] in-flow vibe coffee corner label has no personality
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: the conference table empty-state label for in-flow vibe reads 'reserve the table' (generic) while other vibes have personality: 'no meetings. fix it.' (on-fire), 'everyone is at their desk' (slow-day), 'open office hours' (crushing). Suggested in-flow label: 'sync? book it.' or 'flow time — do not disturb'. Six cycles. One string change.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] cx and orbitR shadow outer-scope constants in ConferenceTable
suggestions.description: Office.jsx ConferenceTable: outer-scope const cx=52, orbitR=38 are redeclared with identical values inside two separate .map() callbacks at lines 925-926 and 943-944. This is a no-shadow lint violation. Remove inner re-declarations and use the outer-scope constants. Ten cycles. This is literally a deletion task.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] plantStageNames array declared but never used
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1088: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is declared inside the Office component body but is never referenced anywhere. plantStage, plantStageEmoji, and plantStageLabel are used — plantStageNames is not. Delete it. Ten cycles. If ESLint is running, this should be throwing a no-unused-vars warning on every build.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] ProgressBar receives updatedAt prop it never uses
suggestions.description: App.jsx: ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt }) destructures updatedAt from props but never references it inside the function body. The only time-related logic uses startedAt for ETA calculation. Remove updatedAt from the destructuring signature and from call sites (line 347). Ten cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] VibeSparkline SVG has both aria-label and title — duplicate accessible name
suggestions.description: App.jsx VibeSparkline line 402-403: svg has aria-label='Vibe history: ...' AND a <title> child with identical content. Same pattern as WallClock (also unfixed). Remove aria-label, keep <title> with role='img'. Two components, one trivial pattern, zero fixes across ten cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] conf-fire-glow SVG filter uses hardcoded DOM ID
suggestions.description: Office.jsx ConferenceTable line 852: filter id='conf-fire-glow' is a hardcoded string. If ConferenceTable were ever rendered more than once, duplicate IDs would break the filter silently in all instances. useId() is already imported — use it: const filterId = useId(); id={filterId}, url(#${filterId}). Ten cycles.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42->...#51] Sparkline legend uses array index as React key
suggestions.description: App.jsx VibeSparkline line 414: history.slice(-3).map((h, i) => <span key={i}>) uses array index as React key. Use h.ts (timestamp) as key instead: key={h.ts}. Prevents unnecessary remounts when vibe history shifts. Ten cycles. One character change.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #46->...#51] formatDuration and formatIdleDuration are near-identical functions
suggestions.description: App.jsx formatDuration and Office.jsx formatIdleDuration implement the same elapsed-time logic with different output prefixes ('Working for Xm' vs bare 'Xm'). Also, getRelativeTime in App.jsx and the inline IIFE in the Ghost OOO bubble make this the third near-duplicate of the same logic across the codebase. Extract to src/utils/time.js as formatElapsedTime(timestamp, prefix='') and import everywhere. Six cycles.
